description: Schedule combat's player-input surface and thread interface (ED-911) as named post-R3 sequence increments. wrapper.engagement() has no player-decision parameter (verified; DELIBERATE sim-first staging per audit refutation R-1) and the R2/R3 sequences reserve no increment for either the input surface or ED-911 (a regression from combat_v30 §10's Leap-in-combat). ACTION: add both as named sequence entries aligned with phase4_5_plan_v1's abilities-as-access layer + the SC-lane walkthrough-first method (ED-IN-0005 policy); do not start before R3 lands (strategic_judgments J-2). RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction on PR #77; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-04-ners-qualitative-audit/ed_options.md E-9 (audit R-1, §4 wire-it #1)). ADDENDUM 2026-07-07 (ED-IN-0024, OPT-14, append-only intent-hygiene correction, C-NERSPESS-5): the "per audit refutation R-1" sourcing is circular — R-1 is itself part of the same PR #77 NERS audit this entry was filed to ratify, so the audit is citing itself as pre-existing ratified basis for the DELIBERATE-staging intent; no pre-audit ED/PP independently establishes that the player-input surface was deliberately deferred to Phase 4. This does not retract the ACTION (scheduling the input surface + ED-911), only flags that its stated intent-provenance is circular.

[RENUMBERED ED-PC-0001 -> ED-PC-0002 at merge, 2026-07-05: the U0 session allocated ED-PC-0001 concurrently against pre-#78 main while PR #78's NERS ratification had already filed ED-PC-0001 (player-input + ED-911 scheduling) — the exact plan-text/counter collision class workplan v6 §6 rules on; renumbered per protocol, code comments citing 'U0/ED-PC-0001' in combat_engine_v1 refer to THIS entry.] U0 units honesty -- first increment of the ratified R3 PC-lane consolidation (designs/audit/2026-07-04-weapon-morphology-granularity/consolidation_v1.md section 4; ungated, dependency spine 'U0 -| nothing'). The scene-combat engine's 0.30 m 'length-unit' indirection is retired: weapons.py head_len/grip_len stored values x0.30 (now honest METRES, all 53 records); weapon_physics.UNIT_M DELETED with every x/UNIT_M site (derive, _gather_len, grip_choke_max, _geom_slide_max_lu -> _geom_slide_max, at_circumstance rear_clearance/geom_slide -- the circumstance bundle's geom_slide member now reports metres); per-length-unit gains rescaled /0.30 (PERC_2H_ARC, LEVER_K, REACH_GEOM_SCALE -- the reach scale absorbs the old unit exactly as consolidation_v1 U0 directs); stored-length constants x0.30 (PERC_GRIP_1H 0.7->0.21 m, GRIP_SHORT 1.0->0.30 m, GRIP_LONG 3.0->0.90 m, LEVER_REF 0.32->0.096 m, REC_GRIP_REF 0.85->0.255 m, and config GRAB_SHORT_REACH_LU=1.25 -> GRAB_SHORT_REACH_M=0.375 -- renamed for name-honesty, readers capabilities.py/contact.py repointed); defense_affinities' wind edge-length saturation 3.0 lu -> 0.90 m; systems.py recoverability tau terms drop WP.UNIT_M. TWO DOCUMENTED DEVIATIONS from the U0 row's compressed summary, both forced by its own binding byte-identity contract: (1) reach_adj is NOT rescaled -- it is a reach-POINTS residual added OUTSIDE REACH_GEOM_SCALE (scaling it x0.30 breaks identity by up to 0.35 reach-pt (0.7x|reach_adj|, roster max paired_short 0.5; figure corrected from 0.32 by the 2026-07-05 Fable adversarial critique) and would break test_reach_base_byte_identical_at_grip_zero's inline formula); (2) PERC_2H_ARC is rescaled though the row omits it (identity forces it once grip_len is metres). ACCEPTANCE MET: built tests/valoria/r3_identity_golden.json PRE-edit (the section-4 process wrapper's OLD-vs-NEW sweep fixture, unit-invariant metres, 53 weapons x grip/room grid x ~60 quantities incl. systems-level reach/tempo/leverage/recovery consumers); new test_combat_units_refactor.py::test_units_refactor_byte_identical passes at 1e-9 (worst observed abs diff 1.8e-15) + test_no_length_unit_indirection_remains (no UNIT_M symbol; rapier 1.14 m / spear 2.01 m honest-metre anchors). Full suite: 8 failed / 168 passed / 1 xfailed -- the accepted-red set unchanged (5 parity reds + 3 named Phase-C reds), zero new red, +2 new green. engine_params JSON re-exported (round-trip --check green); weapon_physics/capabilities/state_graph self-tests green. Golden fixture is REUSED by later byte-identical increments (U3 K=0, U4 parity, U5 grip=0, U6 gate-off, U7 K=0, U8). POST-COMMIT CRITIQUE ADDENDUM (Fable antagonist pass, 2026-07-05, read-only): U0 verified independently (consumer enumeration, algebraic re-derivation, fixture-authenticity reconstruction -- all refutation attempts failed); two residuals FLAGGED as new/latent debt: (1) the frozen Godot port surface (weapon_resource.gd defaults + data/weapons/*.tres, e.g. longsword grip_len=1.6/head_len=2.8) still carries retired length-units while the oracle schema is now metres -- a port<->oracle UNIT divergence in the ED-1050 failure class; the T5 re-export rule covers constants, not the .tres weapon data, so this needs its own re-export line when the port unfreezes; (2) CI's pytest job installs no numpy (valoria-ci.yml:396), so the entire combat suite including test_units_refactor_byte_identical importorskips in CI -- the byte-identity gate is local-only until numpy + an accepted-red manifest land in CI (recommended before U1).

OPT-10, filed per Jordan's 2026-07-07 "ratify all" ruling (ED-IN-0026). Sigma band-discipline unification, executing armature §5.12's ruling: apply the ER-2 continuity correction and pool-aware Overwhelming-bar behavior to the shared resolution substrate everywhere (contest's kernel gains ER-2; combat's fixed 2*Ob bar is replaced with the pool-aware form) -- the symmetric option, direction ruled in §5.12, execution here. Plus mass-battle sigma-copy parity coverage (add to test_sigma_leverage_parity, C-SIG-2) and the Ob=20 dead-code disposition (C-SIG-8). NOTE: touches live resolver math with balance implications -- needs its own verified PR with a regression pass, not a blind landing.

OPT-15, filed per Jordan's 2026-07-07 "ratify all" ruling (ED-IN-0026). ED-1042 reconciliation: flip the steering surfaces to the ledger's strike, and re-file the RESIDUAL drift (derived_stats §4.1 'no Ob penalty ever' vs live ED-1041 bilateral wound-Ob + dead pool_penalty method) as a fresh ED-PC. RESOLVED 2026-07-07 (Stratum-A execution, this PR): flipped the ED-1042 steering surfaces (workplan v6 §5 prose+table, decision_queue item 4) to the ledger strike; re-filed the reverse-direction residual drift as ED-PC-0005.

Reverse-direction wound-Ob prose drift, re-filed from ED-PC-0004 (OPT-15) per the 2026-07-07 unaddressed-areas audit's C-VERIFY ED-1042 section. derived_stats_v30.md §4.1 (AUTHORITATIVE, PP-716) still asserts 'No Ob penalty from wounds, ever', but config.py:65 WOUND_ATK_OB/WOUND_DEF_OB + systems.py:664/693/805 implement the ED-1041 bilateral wound-Ob channel (ratified, approved_by Jordan, resolved 2026-06-22) that directly contradicts it. combatant.py:15/51/77-79 WoundTracker.pool_penalty() (+ WOUND_POOL_PENALTY) remains defined with zero live callers, still tagged '[canonical: derived_stats_v30 §4.1 ... no Ob penalty]' — the dead code ED-1041 item (4) and ED-1042's unexecuted cleanup item (c) both flagged for deletion but neither executed. ACTION: update derived_stats_v30 §4.1's wound-penalty row to describe the live bilateral Ob channel as superseding the old -1D-only rule (authoritative-prose edit — gate on this ED, do not do unilaterally); delete the dead pool_penalty()/WOUND_POOL_PENALTY once confirmed the -1D pool-floor mechanic still fires via a live path. SIM-CODE + authoritative-canon-prose; not a Stratum-A mechanical repair. · UPDATE 2026-07-08 (Stratum-B): sim state CONFIRMED — WoundTracker.pool_penalty() + WOUND_POOL_PENALTY have ZERO live callers repo-wide; the live wound-impairment mechanic is ED-1041's bilateral wound-Ob channel (config.py/systems.py). The dead method was NOT deleted (it is the only -1D-per-wound impl, and whether that rule survives alongside the Ob channel is the very contradiction this ED tracks) — instead the false 'no Ob penalty, ever (canon)' docstrings in combatant.py were corrected to point at ED-1041 + this ED. The AUTHORITATIVE derived_stats_v30 §4.1 (PP-716) prose ('No Ob penalty from wounds, ever' + the -1D pool rule) is UNTOUCHED — reconciling it against ratified ED-1041 is the Jordan call (needs_jordan flipped true). · RESOLVED 2026-07-08 (Jordan ruling, this PR): Jordan ruled "never -1D, always increase fractional Ob" — wounds NEVER impose a -1D pool cut; each wound ALWAYS adds a fractional Ob. ED-1041's bilateral wound-Ob channel (+0.15 attacking / +0.25 defending per wound) fully supersedes the -1D-to-Pools rule, reversing PP-716's -1D unification. Scope confirmed via AskUserQuestion = "Combat now, flag rest": (a) COMBAT executed fully — derived_stats_v30 §4.1 rewritten to the fractional-Ob model (SUPERSEDED PP-716 marker); combat_v30.md §7/§10 + combat_design_v1.md rewritten; combatant.py WOUND_POOL_PENALTY + the dead pool_penalty() DELETED (the felled/incapacitation gate is unaffected — it keys on Health depletion, not pool_penalty); contest_legacy_stub's -1*wounds term removed. (b) NON-COMBAT flagged, not valued: Thread ops, fieldwork, mass-battle Command heads (+ params/core.md/fieldwork.md/mass_combat.md mirrors) had -1D stripped and replaced with 'fractional Ob per wound, value = ED-PC-0006' — NO invented numbers. PP-716 -1D unification recorded superseded in canon/supersession_register.yaml (superseded_by ED-PC-0005). Follow-on non-combat calibration filed as ED-PC-0006.

Non-combat wound fractional-Ob calibration — follow-on to ED-PC-0005 (Jordan ruling 2026-07-08: wounds never -1D, always a fractional Ob). ED-PC-0005 executed COMBAT fully using ED-1041's ratified bilateral channel (+0.15 Ob attacking / +0.25 Ob defending per wound) and flagged the rest per Jordan's "Combat now, flag rest" scope. This ED carries the per-pool fractional-Ob VALUE for every non-combat wound-impaired roll — the sites now reading 'fractional Ob per wound, value ED-PC-0006': Thread operations (Leap/Weaving/Pulling/Mending/FR — threadwork_v30.md, combat_v30.md §10, params/fieldwork.md), physical fieldwork (Endurance-based exploration + Surveil — fieldwork_v30.md §2.2, params/fieldwork.md), and mass-battle Command (CF-wound BG tactic rolls — params/mass_combat.md ED-167). ACTION: sim-calibrate the non-combat per-pool fractional-Ob values (the combat channel is the reference anchor; the pre-ruling '+1 Ob to Leap Thread ops' figure in params/fieldwork.md is a candidate datum to reconcile, not to keep verbatim), then propagate to the flagged heads + their params mirrors and land via a verified PR (merge-ratifies). Also reconcile the two downstream narrative citations of the old -1D wound seed (designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_46_55.md + arcs_46_55_resolved.md, PP-232 framing). NO invented numbers until calibrated. Direction is already ruled (ED-PC-0005); only the values remain. RESOLVED 2026-07-08: adopted +0.15 Ob per wound (cumulative) for all three subsystems, reusing ED-1041's combat 'attacking' magnitude rather than inventing a new figure -- Thread ops, fieldwork (Endurance-exploration/Surveil), and mass-battle Command's CF-wound BG tactic rolls are all active-roller checks with no bilateral attacker/defender split, so the combat channel's +0.25 passive-defence term has no analog and only the +0.15 term transfers. This reconciles the pre-ruling '+1 Ob to Leap' datum (a flat, non-cumulative legacy figure now superseded by the cumulative per-wound value -- gentler at 1 wound, exceeding the old flat figure at 7+ wounds). Propagated to: threadwork_v30.md (3 sites), combat_v30.md section 10 (2 sites), params/fieldwork.md section 2.2-equivalent (2 sites), fieldwork_v30.md section 2.2 (1 site), params/mass_combat.md ED-167 (1 site) + its live implementation sim/cross_scale/zoom_in_out.py (contested_figure_wound_ob changed from a flat int 1 to a float 0.15), derived_stats_v30.md section 4.1 (3 sites, now reads resolved rather than TBD), and the two narrative arc citations (arcs_46_55.md + arcs_46_55_resolved.md -- the Vaynard worked example renarrated from '-2D, roll fails' to '+0.30 Ob (2 wounds), harder roll fails', keeping the existing [UNVERIFIED] leadership-pool caveat untouched since that is a separate, unrelated question). Also found and fixed one residual missed by ED-PC-0005's original pass: combat_design_v1.md section 7 still read '-1D penalty applies' verbatim. supersession_register.yaml's PP-716 entry updated with a RECONCILIATION COMPLETE note and per-file DONE annotations. Full pytest tests/valoria suite re-run after all changes: 196 passed / 8 failed / 1 xfailed -- identical to the pre-existing U1 baseline, zero new regressions (zoom_in_out.py has no dedicated test file and no downstream consumer reads contested_figure_wound_ob yet, confirmed via corpus-wide grep, so the type change int->float carries no other blast radius).
source: Jordan ruling 2026-07-08 (ED-PC-0005 scope: "Combat now, flag rest")

description: RATIFIED subtractive-disposition verdicts for the PC action set (ED-IN-0027 pessimist audit; decision ratified, EXECUTION is this PC-lane follow-up). Feint DISTILL (into the continuous commit mechanic that already narrates it, wrapper.py:140 / WS-5); Establish Distance + Escape MERGE (one disengage contest — no differentiated resolution text); Disarm + Tie Up + Retrieve MERGE (one Contact-axis control verb branching on outcome, per the ratified I7b/D8-D9 Contact node); Full Guard + Dodge DISTILL (base offence/defence pool-split; Templar 'Shield of Faith' re-keys to the 0-Offence state check); Rescue REFINE (drop accreted chain-block/incap carve-outs); Take a Breath REFINE (re-home the mutual-rest-stalemate fix to scene/encounter pacing, not the action's own clause); Stunt REFINE (bind to the fixed-geometry Stunt vocabulary, combat_v30:372 — resolves the no-GM open-improvisation conflict, ED-1061 precedent). Retires/shrinks: module_contracts combat.feint + combat.grapple PENDING rows, sim ACTION_RESOLUTION_ORDER duplicates, combat_v30 §4 ACTIONS table rows (net -5 resolver rows). Docket rows: ed_options.md PC section. · DEFERRED 2026-07-08 (Jordan: 'Defer PC' — for personal combat only designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/ is up to date; the rest is stale). The verdicts here judged the discrete combat_v30 §4 ACTIONS menu, which is PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED (ED-900, 2026-06-04): its resolution layer is replaced by the canonical continuous resolver combat_engine_v1/, which has no discrete player-action surface yet (sim/personal/combat.py is the deprecated file). The subtractive consolidations (Feint DISTILL, Escape/Establish-Distance MERGE, Disarm/Tie-Up/Retrieve MERGE, Full-Guard/Dodge DISTILL) are already realized by / moot under combat_engine_v1's continuous design -- the continuous commit mechanic subsumes Feint, the ratified Contact node (I7b/D8-D9) subsumes Disarm/Tie-Up/Retrieve, the base offence/defence pool-split subsumes Full-Guard/Dodge -- so the audit retroactively VALIDATED the move to combat_engine_v1 rather than finding live work on a current surface. NO edits were made to the stale combat_v30 §4 or sim/personal/combat.py. The forward constraints (do NOT re-introduce these as separate discrete verbs if/when a player layer is built on combat_engine_v1; plus the Rescue/Take-a-Breath/Stunt refinements) attach to ED-PC-0001 (the post-R3 player-input surface). Re-open only if combat_engine_v1 grows a discrete player-action menu that reintroduces the over-articulation.

R3 U1 -- PoB (point-of-balance) recalibration, executes JD-1 (consolidation_v1.md section 6): Jordan ruled "accept plan bands" (2026-07-08) -- the plan's own default arms-scholarship ranges (rapier 3-11cm, greatsword 8-20cm, 1H swords 6-14cm, poleaxe-class polearms 20-55cm forward, staff ~0). weapons.py data-only mass redistribution for the 6 weapons V7's evidence table flagged as systematically biased: rapier (17.0->9.0cm, blade 0.62->0.455kg / pommel 0.348->0.513kg), arming (17.8->11.0cm, blade 0.78->0.645kg / pommel 0.234->0.369kg), longsword (19.4->13.9cm, blade 0.87->0.755kg / pommel 0.3->0.415kg -- the specific value chosen so systems.recoverability_factor(longsword) lands at 0.98, within test_anchor_is_near_one's existing 0.03 tolerance of the untouched REC_I_REF/REC_S_REF anchors), greatsword (30.4->18.0cm, blade 1.75->1.414kg / pommel 0.548->0.884kg), bec_de_corbin (5.1->22.0cm, hammer/beak/spike head scaled 2.098x 0.58->1.217kg, haft 1.5834->0.9454kg), lucerne_hammer (7.2->24.0cm, head scaled 1.92x 0.70->1.344kg, haft 1.5834->0.9394kg -- matching poleaxe's own physical scale, since it shares poleaxe's head_len/grip_len/composite-head shape). Every weapon's total mass is unchanged (pure redistribution). weapon_physics.HEFT_REF re-derived from longsword's new PoB (0.1545336822851806 -> 0.09609384729140724) to preserve heft(longsword)==1.0 by construction; this rescales heft() for every weapon uniformly (a deliberate re-baseline). Cinquedea (also in V7's evidence table, PoB -1.8cm) was DELIBERATELY NOT touched -- JD-1 named no band for it and inventing one was out of scope. Fixtures regenerated with recorded reasons per consolidation_v1's process wrapper: tests/valoria/r3_identity_golden.json (53 weapons; only the 6 flagged weapons' derive/agility/defense_affinities move for real, every other weapon's heft-family fields move by the same HEFT_REF rescale, confirmed via diff), tests/valoria/golden_heft_percussion_snapshot.json (full re-dump). New test tests/valoria/test_combat_pob_bands.py::test_pob_within_realistic_range pins the JD-1 bands going forward. Retired 2 accepted-red tests as the plan predicted (test_anchor_is_near_one, test_lunge_quality_is_weapon_derived_continuous). NEW FINDING (not predicted by the plan, fully documented rather than silently patched): the correctly-banded arming/longsword now read BELOW spear's own untouched heft numerator even at each band's ceiling (checked exhaustively) -- test_falsifiable_heft_ordering and its test_combat_invariants.py duplicate test_heft_percussion_ordering_at_ideal now fail on the spear<arming term. This is NOT a new defect -- it corroborates the already-tracked HANDOFF_PC.md 'SPEAR flat-dominance' finding via a second symptom, and per the same convention as the existing test_gap_game_poleaxe_spikes_plate [PHASE-C FLAG] both tests are left deliberately failing with the finding recorded in their docstrings, not patched away. A second, POSITIVE side effect: lucerne_hammer's corrected (heavier, poleaxe-scale) head now makes its blunt percussion authority dominant enough that it joins poleaxe's existing [PHASE-C FLAG] exclusion from test_use_mode_selection_emerges_from_primitives' mode-switching set (updated, was previously listed as switching under the old under-massed head). Net accepted-red count: 8 -> 8 (2 retired, 2 new PHASE-C-flagged, both pre-existing entries -- test_gap_game_poleaxe_spikes_plate and the 5 test_combat_element_parity.py Phase-A fixture-schema-drift tests -- untouched, confirmed out of U1 scope). references/engine_params/combat_engine_v1.json unaffected (config.py untouched; verified via export_engine_params.py --check, zero drift). 196 passed / 8 failed / 1 xfailed, tools/valoria_local.py --staged clean. NEXT: U2 (graded mode affordance + Phase-C percussion enactment, ED-PC-0008 at implementation) is now unblocked (U2 <- U1). · RENUMBERED 2026-07-08 at merge time: originally filed as ED-PC-0007, which independently collided with main's own ED-PC-0007 allocation (pessimist-audit PC action-consolidation deferral, above) — same plan-label-vs-allocation collision class as this same PR's ED-PC-0001/ED-PC-0005 precedent notes. Main's ED-PC-0007 was already merged to main first, so it keeps the number; this entry (not yet merged) moves to the next free PC-lane slot, ED-PC-0010. All internal citations/comments updated to match (weapons.py, weapon_physics.py, tests, CURRENT.md, consolidation_v1.md, HANDOFF_PC.md, id_reservations.yaml).

U2 scoping findings -- attempted U2 (graded mode affordance + Phase-C percussion enactment, consolidation_v1.md section 4) this session, immediately after U1/ED-PC-0010 landed and unblocked it. Found U2's one-line spec ('drop cut_factor 0.45 floor, per-primitive gates MODE_EDGE_MIN/MODE_TIP_MIN/MODE_PERC_MIN, wire geo[thrust], drop percussion blunt self-gate, fix the 8.0 cap/mace-pin inversion') materially underspecified for a safe implementation -- the missing source plan (A: /root/.claude/plans/fuzzy-toasting-wombat.md, not present in this environment) evidently carried detail consolidation_v1.md's summary table did not preserve. THREE new findings, each requiring its own Jordan-reviewed default before U2 can safely land (none implemented this pass -- no risky guess committed to engine code):

(1) PERCUSSION SELF-GATE REMOVAL PRODUCES A SEVERE BALANCE REGRESSION, NOT 'ships weak' (JD-4's own framing). Measured directly: naively dropping weapon_physics.percussion_authority's head-not-blunt-returns-zero gate and letting every weapon's own mass*PoB_frac*energy_credit feed the existing PERC_SCALE=9.5/PERC_EXP=0.30 formula gives greatsword=6.12, katana=6.26, longsword=5.77 -- comparable to or exceeding poleaxe's own 7.48 and close to mace's 8.0 cap. Root cause: PERC_SCALE/PERC_EXP were fit ONLY against dedicated blunt weapons (whose entire forward mass IS the striking surface); a sword's percussion-capable mass is specifically its small rear pommel, not its whole blade+PoB -- the existing whole-weapon formula has no way to distinguish these cases. JD-4's 'ships weak' default cannot be honored by a bare self-gate removal; a genuinely NEW pommel-specific percussion sub-model (grounded per this repo's own combat-grounding-methodology: derive from the pommel's own mass_kg/x_m via percussion_element_authority, not the whole-weapon formula) is needed first. NOT implemented.

(2) weapon_physics.thrust_factor (geometry.py) carries its OWN undocumented floor bug, structurally identical to cut_factor's already-flagged 0.45 floor: its formula (0.35 + 0.65*point_concentration) times a cross_section term never reaches 0 even at point_concentration=0 (a broad, pointless face). Measured: mace (pc=0.02, pure blunt face) scores thrust=0.34; staff scores 0.31 -- both nontrivial 'thrust' readings for weapons with no point at all, purely from their rigid cross_section. This forces any MODE_TIP_MIN choice into an uncomfortably narrow band (0.34-0.37) to satisfy the plan's own test_mace_staff_no_thrust (must exclude mace 0.34/staff 0.31) AND test_greatsword_katana_sabre_afford_thrust (must include sabre, whose thrust=0.37 clears that same window by only 0.03) -- a fragile margin this repo's own anti-sim-fit principle (Jordan memory: combat-grounding-methodology) warns against accepting silently. Proposed default (not applied): drop thrust_factor's own floor the same way U2 already drops cut_factor's, widening the margin -- but this is an ADDITIONAL formula change beyond consolidation_v1's literal text and needs the same sign-off as JD-1 was, not a silent bundle.

(3) The graded-affordance change (decoupling element_afforded's per-mode gating from the head category) has a MUCH wider blast radius than the plan's 5 named acceptance tests. Verified directly: test_combat_invariants.py already carries exact-set assertions for composite weapons (test_afforded_heads_emerge_from_phase_b2_mode_elements: ji=={point,curved_cut}, guisarme=={cut_thrust,point}, voulge, bec_de_corbin, lucerne_hammer, goedendag, poleaxe) that a straightforward head-independent secondary cut/point check WOULD break -- e.g. ji's point-tokened spearhead element carries edge_keenness=0.40 in its own per-mode_element geometry, comfortably clearing a MODE_EDGE_MIN~=0.15 floor and adding a THIRD 'cut' token to a set the existing test pins at exactly two. Landing U2 correctly requires re-deriving the expected new set for every composite weapon in the roster (the same rigor as a fixture regeneration, but with no existing 'right answer' to check against), not just the 5 single-mode weapons the plan names.

RECOMMENDATION: resolve (1) and (2) as their own Jordan-reviewed forks (proposed as JD-9 and JD-10 respectively, extending consolidation_v1.md section 6) before attempting U2's implementation; (3) is a scope/effort note for whoever implements U2, not a design fork. NOTHING in geometry.py/systems.py/weapon_physics.py was changed this session -- all three findings were verified by direct interactive measurement against the live post-U1 tree, not applied.
source: designs/audit/2026-07-04-weapon-morphology-granularity/consolidation_v1.md section 4 (U2 row); direct measurement against the post-U1 working tree, 2026-07-08

description:
JD-4 + JD-9 DETERMINED (Jordan directive 2026-07-08: 'determine both by testing bottom-up emergent primitives and validating top-down against history and hema and physics'), following on ED-PC-0008's scoping findings. Two formula-level fixes, both grounded and tested; the LIVE mode-selection wiring was tried, found unsafe, and explicitly reverted (see below) -- not silently forced through.

JD-9 (thrust_factor's own floor bug): geometry.thrust_factor's additive point_concentration floor (0.35 + 0.65*pc) is dropped, matching cut_factor's already-applied fix -- pressure = force/area, so a broad pointless face must read ~0 thrust regardless of cross_section rigidity. Verified against the full roster: mace/staff drop from 0.34/0.31 to 0.02/0.04; the needle-class thrusters correctly stay near-zero on the (also-fixed) cut_factor side; the previously fragile 0.34-0.37 MODE_TIP_MIN window widens to a clean gap (mace/staff ~0.02-0.04 vs sabre/katana/greatsword 0.26+). New MODE_EDGE_MIN=0.15 / MODE_TIP_MIN=0.15 constants defined in systems.py (matched, symmetric pair) for future graded-affordance work. element_afforded's cut_thrust atomic branch now compares cut against geo['thrust'] instead of geo['gap'] (the literal 'wire geo[thrust]' ask) -- gap is kept threaded separately for the downstream armour-gap math, unaffected. This narrow swap (one existing token's internal score, no new competing token) was fully re-validated: zero new test failures.

JD-4 (pommel/Mordhau percussion ceiling): researched HEMA sourcing (Wikipedia 'Mordhau (weaponry)'; Malevus 'Mordhau: The Murder Stroke Technique'; corroborating Quora sourcing on comparative effectiveness) -- Mordhau/Mordschlag is a documented, attested half-sword technique: both hands move onto the blade (HEMA: 'one hand towards the tip...the other towards the crossguard'), and the guard+pommel assembly, now projecting outward, is swung like an improvised mace -- used specifically when cuts/thrusts fail against rigid armour, explicitly framed across sources as a supplementary technique, 'far less injurious' than a dedicated mace/warhammer against rigid plate (real concussive threat retained against mail/padding/an exposed head). New weapon_physics.hilt_assembly_mass(w) (guard+pommel+haft, the part that becomes the striking head) and reversed_grip_percussion(w) (bottom-up: reuses the EXISTING percussion_element_authority per-element form -- the same physics already applied to a lucerne_hammer's rear fluke -- with hilt_assembly_mass as the striking mass and the weapon's own grip_len as the lever arm, gated to hands==2 bladed weapons only per the sourced material). percussion_authority's non-blunt self-gate now routes to this instead of a hard 0.0. CALIBRATION FINDING (measured, not guessed): percussion_authority's own PERC_SCALE=9.5/PERC_EXP=0.30 power-law cannot express 'structurally weak' from input magnitude alone -- verified across many input choices (whole hilt mass at head_len, at grip_len, pommel-only mass at a near-zero lever arm) that EVERY combination saturates toward 5-7/8, because a fractional exponent compresses any nonzero input toward the upper band. An explicit REVERSED_GRIP_EFFICIENCY=0.25 discount [FIAT, HEMA-grounded direction, open to playtesting] is applied on top of the raw located-part computation as the honest alternative to either leaving Mordhau mace-strength (contradicting every source's 'far less injurious' framing) or hand-fudging PERC_SCALE/PERC_EXP themselves (which would distort every dedicated blunt weapon's own calibration). Result: two-handed swords read 1.4-1.8/8, clearly weak vs mace's 8.0/poleaxe's ~7.5, gated to exactly 0.0 for one-handed swords/daggers (no comparable technique attested) and hafted weapons (already have a dedicated head).

INTEGRATION FINDING (measured, then reverted -- a real architectural gap, not routed around): wiring reversed_grip_percussion into element_afforded as a competing 'blunt' token was implemented and tested, then reverted after discovering select_mode's greedy comparator (core.coupling) does NOT read percussion authority's magnitude except specifically against mail/plate armour (_transmit's rigid-armour dampening term) -- DELIVERY['blunt']=1.6 is a FIXED constant, the highest of any token. Verified directly: every eligible two-handed sword's weak Mordhau option incorrectly WON selection against NONE/light armour (backwards from the historical grounding -- Mordhau is attested as a response to armour defeating the edge, not a general preference) and only correctly lost to the native cut/thrust at medium/heavy, where the mail/plate dampening finally applies. core.damage()'s own 'heft = 3.0*(perc/8.0)' term DOES correctly scale by the weak magnitude -- the bug is specifically in the SELECTION step (core.coupling), not the eventual damage. Fixing core.coupling itself (making DELIVERY-equivalent scale with percussion magnitude for all materials, not just mail/plate) is a separate, real engine fix this session did not have scope to make safely -- filing it as a new fork rather than silently routing around it via a narrower/hackier gate. Separately, the independent cut/point secondary-affordance checks (the OTHER half of 'graded mode affordance', tried alongside the percussion wiring) were also reverted: they turn 7 roster armour-tier 'changers' into 27 -- e.g. bear_spear (head='point') newly out-scores its own point with an incidental cut, breaking test_thrust_protection_grip_invariant -- a roster-wide re-validation this session did not have room to complete responsibly (matches ED-PC-0008 finding 3).

Net: JD-4 and JD-9 are DETERMINED and correct at the formula level, each with new direct unit tests (tests/valoria/test_combat_reversed_grip.py, 6 tests) exercising the actual grounded functions. Fixtures regenerated (r3_identity_golden.json, golden_heft_percussion_snapshot.json -- percussion_authority/puncture_pressure now nonzero for every hands==2 bladed weapon, all other weapons and every other quantity unchanged, confirmed via diff). Live mode-selection wiring remains a clearly-documented open item (systems.py's element_afforded docstring; MODE_EDGE_MIN/MODE_TIP_MIN/MODE_PERC_MIN constants stay defined for whoever picks it up alongside the core.coupling fix). 202 passed / 8 failed (identical pre-existing set, zero new regressions) / 1 xfailed; tools/valoria_local.py clean; 0 ED-citation violations.
source: Jordan directive 2026-07-08 ('determine both by testing bottom-up emergent primitives and validating top-down against history and hema and physics'); Wikipedia 'Mordhau (weaponry)'; Malevus 'Mordhau: The Murder Stroke Technique'; direct measurement against the post-U1/ED-PC-0008 working tree

description: Live mode-selection wiring for JD-4 (Mordhau reversed-grip percussion) + JD-9 (thrust_factor floor drop) -- the integration ED-PC-0009 determined at the formula level but explicitly did NOT wire live, after finding select_mode's comparator (core.coupling) ignored percussion magnitude except vs mail/plate armour. RESOLVED 2026-07-08. Two engine fixes landed in core.py/systems.py, both validated via a 13-agent agonist/antagonist adversarial Workflow (6 Sonnet producer/critic weapon-group pairs cross-examining each other's HEMA/physics grounding against the live engine + 1 Opus final synthesis) against the full 53-weapon roster, per Jordan's explicit request for that methodology this session: (1) PERCUSSION: core.py's FIX-1b percussion-authority transmit scaling in _transmit() extended from `mat in ('mail','plate')` to every material, so the weak Mordhau candidate (perc ~1.4-1.8) correctly loses to a weapon's own cut/thrust vs soft targets and is only competitive vs rigid armour it cannot otherwise defeat -- matching the HEMA sourcing (Mordhau as a response to armour defeating the edge, not a general preference). FIRST ATTEMPT collapsed none/cloth and mail/plate onto the SAME reference (PERC_AUTH_REF=8.0, mace's exact value); an adversarial review caught this as wrong -- it silently discounted every other dedicated hammer-class weapon too (poleaxe 7.61, lucerne_hammer 7.05, bec_de_corbin 6.51) and FLIPPED bec_de_corbin/lucerne_hammer's selected mode away from their own hammer face at the UNARMOURED tier (backwards -- their hammer face should win vs bare flesh). Fixed with a dual-reference split: mail/plate keeps PERC_AUTH_REF=8.0 UNCHANGED (preserves the pre-existing, already-tested calibration exactly, including poleaxe's spike-vs-plate gap game -- verified byte-identical to pre-session behaviour); none/cloth uses the lower PERC_AUTH_REF_SOFT=6.5, anchored on the weakest attested dedicated hammer (bec_de_corbin) rather than the strongest (mace). Verified directly: all four dedicated hammer-class weapons (mace, goedendag, poleaxe, bec_de_corbin, lucerne_hammer) select their own hammer face at every tier they did before this session's changes, at both reference points.(2) CUT MAGNITUDE: core.coupling's DELIVERY constants did not scale with a candidate's own derived cut/thrust magnitude the way percussion now does -- an incidental secondary 'cut' token (e.g. rapier, head='point', geo['cut']=0.30) could outscore the weapon's own dedicated 'point' (geo['gap']=0.74) purely because DELIVERY['cut']=1.5 > DELIVERY['point']=1.45, regardless of how weak the incidental edge actually was -- the original reported bug this session set out to fix. Fixed via CUT_AUTH_REF=0.70 (anchored on the weakest attested genuinely-dedicated cutter, hook_sword, not a roster-wide average/median -- verified no weapon in the roster is natively headed 'cut', so the scaling carries zero regression risk to any existing native selection), scaling DELIVERY['cut'] by min(1.0, eff/CUT_AUTH_REF). 'point' needed no analogous fix for the DIRECTLY-reported bug pattern: DELIVERY['point']=1.45 is already the lowest shear-family-adjacent constant, so a secondary point token can never beat a native cutter purely on the constant. `eff` threaded through select_mode's widened return tuple (6th element, sel_eff) and core.strike so it also scales the eventual damage coupling, not just selection -- explicitly scoped to the coupling factor only, never touching the separately-deferred weight-class heft term (plan #9).ROSTER-WIDE VALIDATION (the adversarial Workflow): confirmed the original bug is fixed (rapier, spear, yari, bear_spear, dangpa, main_gauche and every other native point-headed weapon now stays correctly on 'point' at every armour tier). Confirmed the re-enabled graded secondary-affordance checks (percussion/cut/point) expand the roster-wide 'changers' set (weapons whose selected head varies by armour tier) from 7 to 17, with the ten new ones judged historically/physically defensible (greatsword/katana/tachi/nandao/glaive/guandao/podao/fauchard/hook_sword's secondary half-sword thrust; ji/kama_yari's incidental spearhead edge). tests/valoria/test_combat_invariants.py's test_use_mode_selection_emerges_from_primitives and test_afforded_heads_emerge_from_phase_b2_mode_elements updated accordingly with the adversarial pass's per-weapon reasoning. ALSO SURFACED a SECOND, related, NOT-yet-fixed gap on the 'point' side specifically for the one-handed sabre-class roster (sabre/scimitar/falchion) -- filed separately as ED-PC-0012 rather than rushed through in this same session (a fix would ripple into several of the newly-accepted two-handed cutters and needs its own roster-wide re-verification). tests/valoria/test_combat_capabilities.py::test_pure_cutters_have_no_gates updated: greatsword removed (legitimately gained real capability), sabre kept and left deliberately failing, documenting the ED-PC-0012 bug rather than silently patching around it.No fixtures needed regeneration (r3_identity_golden.json/golden_heft_percussion_snapshot.json record weapon_physics.py-level derivations -- heft/percussion_authority -- untouched by this ED's core.py/systems.py-level selection-logic changes). Full suite: 210 passed / 9 failed / 1 xpassed -- 8 pre-existing documented failures (5 Phase-A fixture-schema-drift + 2 spear-dominance + poleaxe's pre-existing Phase-C mass-model gap-game issue, all confirmed unaffected/byte-identical to pre-session behaviour) + the one new, fully-documented sabre failure (ED-PC-0012). The xpass is a pre-existing, unrelated mass-battle test. tools/valoria_local.py, tools/validate_ed_citations.py, tools/freshness_gate.py, tools/ci_co_file_checker.py all clean.
source: designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/core.py + systems.py (direct code); ED-PC-0009 ledger entry (the deferred integration item this resumes); agonist/antagonist adversarial Workflow (13 agents, 2026-07-08, per Jordan's explicit request for that methodology)

description: Found via the ED-PC-0011 agonist/antagonist roster-wide adversarial validation Workflow (13 agents: 6 Sonnet producer verdicts x 6 Sonnet independent critics x 1 Opus synthesis) — a SECOND, structurally identical gap to the one ED-PC-0011 fixed for the 'cut' token, this time on 'point': core.coupling's DELIVERY['point']=1.45 constant does not scale by the candidate's own derived thrust magnitude (geo['thrust']/gap_precision), and core._transmit's puncture path's gap-seeking term (max(t, GAP_EXPOSURE[mat]*gap_prec)) is FLOOR-LOCKED to the raw material transmission `t` for any physically-plausible gap_prec below ~1.0 -- verified directly: scimitar (point geo=0.16), sabre (0.26), falchion (0.23), and hook_sword (0.40) ALL score the IDENTICAL coupling=1.2760 for their secondary point token at 'light' armour, because 0.85*gap_prec never exceeds t=0.88 for any of these gap_prec values -- the geometry has ZERO effect on the transmit term at this tier, only the fixed DELIVERY constant and material RESIST do. Since a native cutter's own shear-mode transmit degrades hard with armour (1.5 -> 0.93 -> 0.42 -> 0.29 across none/light/medium/heavy) while point's transmit never drops below roughly 0.4-0.9 regardless of the point's actual geometric quality, EVERY cutting weapon with ANY secondary point token above MODE_TIP_MIN eventually switches to 'point' at some tier -- the switch point is driven mostly by the fixed constants, not genuine per-weapon geometry. The roster-wide adversarial pass judged this DOES apply meaningfully to the one-handed sabre-class roster (sabre, scimitar, falchion): their historically-attested identity is dedicated slashing technique with poor thrusting geometry (scimitar's point=0.16 is the lowest of any weapon in the roster), and their new tier-switch to 'point' was judged a floor-locked artifact, not earned capability -- FLAG-AS-FORK, not accepted as a validated finding. hook_sword's switch (point=0.40) was judged more measurably above-floor via its own gap_precision and was accepted with a caveat rather than forked. The two-handed cutter roster (greatsword/katana/tachi/nandao/glaive/guandao/podao/fauchard, all with point in 0.24-0.57) was judged historically defensible regardless (half-sword thrust-to-gap as a primary technique against armour is well-attested HEMA doctrine for these classes) and accepted as genuine changers, though the synthesis noted the underlying mechanism narrative needs care (the FIX-1b-style RESIST/DELIVERY interaction, not a `gap_precision` vs `thrust_factor` conflation the producer/critic relay caught mixing across weapons). RECOMMENDATION (not implemented this pass, deliberately deferred rather than rushed): a THRUST_AUTH_REF constant analogous to CUT_AUTH_REF, anchored on the weakest attested NATIVE pointer in the roster (bec_de_corbin's point element, 0.370, per the same weakest-of-the-genuine-population principle CUT_AUTH_REF/PERC_AUTH_REF_SOFT already use) so no native point-headed weapon is affected, scaling DELIVERY['point'] by min(1.0, eff/THRUST_AUTH_REF) exactly as 'cut' now is. Preliminary check: this would correctly discount scimitar (0.16/0.37=0.43), sabre (0.26/0.37=0.70), falchion (0.23/0.37=0.62), while leaving hook_sword (0.40/0.37, clamps to 1.0) and every two-handed cutter with point>=0.37 unaffected -- but SOME two-handed cutters (tachi 0.32, nandao 0.24, glaive 0.29, podao 0.24) sit BELOW 0.37 too and would also see some discount, which could shift their own selected-sequence tier-switch points (possibly toward their Mordhau/blunt option earlier for tachi/nandao) and needs its OWN roster-wide re-verification pass before landing -- exactly the kind of ripple this ED defers rather than rushes through without validation. NOT implemented; core.py/systems.py unchanged by this ED. Also flagged by the same pass, lower priority: voulge's rear thrusting-heel-spike element has edge_keenness=0.15, landing EXACTLY on MODE_EDGE_MIN=0.15 (the strict '>' check is the only thing preventing a spurious 'cut' token) -- verify this authored value's provenance against its cited source rather than accepting a value that coincidentally equals the gate constant, and/or add a regression test pinning that voulge affords no 'cut' token.
source: Agonist/antagonist adversarial Workflow (13 agents), 2026-07-08 — designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/core.py direct measurement

description: Bundle of three attribute/value coherence audit (ED-IN-0029) items, decision-ratified, execution deferred to the PC lane (+MB co-sign where noted): (1) OPT-AV-15 RESIST re-export -- combat_config.gd carries pre-2026-06-30 values, 4/12 cells drifted (census C6-M1); GAP_EXPOSURE/GAP_PREC_REF never carried (C6-M2). RATIFIED: re-export from core.py now per the ED-1050 rule (fix canon, then re-export -- already satisfied on the oracle side); annotate the .gd as stale until the port slice actually lands. (2) OPT-AV-17 exporter scope fence -- tools/export_engine_params.py covers only a subset of core.py's canonical constants (census C6-F2/F3). RATIFIED: widen the exporter to cover core.py's ~15 canonical constants (small count, co-equal canon, the exporter's own docstring already claims the whole typed oracle) rather than merely documenting the gap; prune the 7 dead CFG keys at the next config.py pass, matching the existing DAMAGE_SCALE precedent. (3) wound-penalty sweep completion (combat_v30.md:307 + mass_battle_v30.md:313-314, OPT-AV-7 hygiene batch item, MB co-sign -- not executed by the IN-only hygiene pass since it is a lane-owned file touch). Decision-ratified, execution pending.
